properties: Nephelium lappaceum, also known as the rambutan, is a plant species with several therapeutic properties. side_effects: Nephelium lappaceum, also known as the rambutan, has been reported to cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. intro: Nephelium lappaceum, commonly known as the Rambutan tree, is a tropical fruit-bearing plant native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Nephelium lappaceum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nephelium lappaceum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support immune function and overall well-being.
